# Captain Mobile — Code Review

*Generated by uber-review on 2026-06-17 — full scope — first run*

## 1. Executive Summary

Captain is a single-user React Native/Expo voice+text assistant that is functionally sound but has several robustness gaps clustered around two areas: async lifecycle management in TTS playback (promises that can hang and orphan audio objects) and unguarded `JSON.parse` of persisted state that can crash screens on launch. None of the findings are exploitable security issues given the single-user threat model, but the hung-promise and corrupt-storage paths are realistic crash/hang vectors worth fixing before relying on the app daily.

## 2. Finding Count

| Severity | Count |
|----------|-------|
| HIGH     | 6     |
| MEDIUM   | 5     |
| LOW      | 3     |
| Dep-Vuln | 4     |
| **Total**| **18**|

## 3. Findings

### HIGH

| # | Location | Issue | Confidence | Est. Fix |
|---|----------|-------|------------|----------|
| H1 | voice.js:59-67 | TTS playback Promise only resolves on `didJustFinish`; on audio error/corrupt blob/silent session failure it hangs forever, so `speak()` never returns | HIGH | 20 min |
| H2 | ChatScreen.js:141-145 | `isSpeaking` stuck true and `handleSend` coroutine leaked when `speak()` hangs (consequence of H1; resolved by fixing H1) | MEDIUM | covered by H1 |
| H3 | ChatScreen.js:43 | `JSON.parse` of saved messages with no try/catch; corrupt storage throws unhandled rejection in async IIFE, breaking screen on launch | HIGH | 10 min |
| H4 | SettingsScreen.js:52 | Same unguarded `JSON.parse` for settings; crashes settings screen on corrupt storage | HIGH | 10 min |
| H5 | SettingsScreen.js:107 | Credentials written to AsyncStorage *before* `testConnection()`; failed test leaves bad key/URL persisted as next-launch default | HIGH | 15 min |
| H6 | ChatScreen.js:88, 72 | Mic button has no `disabled`/`isProcessing` guard; speech `result` handler calls `handleSend` directly with no guard, allowing concurrent in-flight API streams | HIGH | 20 min |

### MEDIUM

| # | Location | Issue | Confidence | Est. Fix |
|---|----------|-------|------------|----------|
| M1 | ChatScreen.js:132 | Final-message AsyncStorage write is fire-and-forget inside setState callback; crash between stream-end and write loses last message | MEDIUM | 15 min |
| M2 | ChatScreen.js:139,152 | Corrupt settings `JSON.parse` inside try/catch but the catch calls `sendMessage(userText)`, re-sending an already-completed message (duplicate response) | MEDIUM | 10 min |
| M3 | ChatScreen.js:138 | `setIsProcessing(false)` fires before TTS starts; `handleTextSend` doesn't check `isSpeaking`, so a new text send can start a concurrent stream during playback | MEDIUM | 10 min |
| M4 | voice.js (speakWithElevenLabs) | New sound assigned to `currentSound` without stopping previous; old sound orphaned, its callback nulls the NEW sound's reference | MEDIUM | 15 min |
| M5 | voice.js:86-93 | `cancelSpeech` nulls `currentSound` before async `stopAsync`/`unloadAsync` complete, risking orphaned playback/reference races | MEDIUM | covered by M4 |

### LOW

| # | Location | Issue | Confidence | Est. Fix |
|---|----------|-------|------------|----------|
| L1 | api.js:17,105 / voice.js:30 / SettingsScreen.js:56,135 | Self-issued API key hardcoded as plaintext fallback in 5 places, committed to git | n/a | 15 min (rotate) |
| L2 | api.js:103-104 | `sendMessageStream` strips 192.168 URL in-memory but doesn't `removeItem`; stale URL persists until `getClient()` runs | n/a | 5 min |
| L3 | ChatScreen.js:116,187 | `[...messages]` snapshot instead of functional update; only manifests under concurrent-send conditions | n/a | covered by H6 |

### Dependency Vulnerabilities (npm audit)

| Severity | Package | Issue | Runtime Impact |
|----------|---------|-------|----------------|
| CRITICAL | shell-quote | Newline injection in object `.op` values | Dev/build only — not in runtime |
| HIGH | axios | Prototype pollution via `validateStatus` merge | Runtime — upgrade to 1.7.x+ |
| HIGH | ws | Uninitialized memory disclosure | Dev/build transitive only |
| HIGH | form-data | CRLF injection | Dev transitive only |

---

## 4. Fix Recommendations (exact code changes)

### H1 — voice.js:59-67 — Add watchdog + error path to TTS Promise

```javascript
// BEFORE
return new Promise((resolve) => {
  sound.setOnPlaybackStatusUpdate((status) => {
    if (status.didJustFinish) {
      sound.unloadAsync();
      currentSound = null;
      resolve();
    }
  });
});

// AFTER
return new Promise((resolve) => {
  let settled = false;
  const finish = () => {
    if (settled) return;
    settled = true;
    sound.unloadAsync().catch(() => {});
    if (currentSound === sound) currentSound = null;
    clearTimeout(watchdog);
    resolve();
  };
  const watchdog = setTimeout(finish, 60000); // hard ceiling; adjust if clips exceed 60s
  sound.setOnPlaybackStatusUpdate((status) => {
    if (!status.isLoaded && status.error) { finish(); return; }
    if (status.didJustFinish) { finish(); }
  });
});
```

This also resolves H2 (speak() now always returns) and the M4/M5 audio-object races (currentSound nulled only if it still points at this sound).

### H3 — ChatScreen.js:43 — Guard parse with try/catch

```javascript
// BEFORE
if (saved) setMessages(JSON.parse(saved));

// AFTER
if (saved) {
  try {
    const parsed = JSON.parse(saved);
    if (Array.isArray(parsed)) setMessages(parsed);
  } catch {
    await AsyncStorage.removeItem(STORAGE_KEY); // clear corrupt data; start clean
  }
}
```

### H4 — SettingsScreen.js:52 — Same guard

```javascript
// BEFORE
if (saved) setSettings({ ...DEFAULT_SETTINGS, ...JSON.parse(saved) });

// AFTER
if (saved) {
  try {
    setSettings({ ...DEFAULT_SETTINGS, ...JSON.parse(saved) });
  } catch {
    setSettings(DEFAULT_SETTINGS);
    await AsyncStorage.removeItem(SETTINGS_KEY);
  }
}
```

### H5 — SettingsScreen.js:107 — Test before persisting; roll back on failure

```javascript
// BEFORE
await AsyncStorage.multiSet([['captain_api_url', apiUrl], ['captain_api_key', apiKey]]);
resetClient();
const ok = await testConnection();
setStatus(ok ? 'connected' : 'failed');

// AFTER
const prev = await AsyncStorage.multiGet(['captain_api_url', 'captain_api_key']);
await AsyncStorage.multiSet([['captain_api_url', apiUrl], ['captain_api_key', apiKey]]);
resetClient();
const ok = await testConnection();
if (!ok) {
  await AsyncStorage.multiSet(prev.map(([k, v]) => [k, v ?? '']));
  resetClient();
}
setStatus(ok ? 'connected' : 'failed');
```

> **Decision needed:** Alternative is to not persist at all in `handleTest` (test-only flow) and require a separate Save button press. Either approach works — see §7 for UX implications.

### H6 — ChatScreen.js:72 + 88 — useRef guard for concurrent sends

```javascript
// Add near top of ChatScreen component:
const inFlightRef = useRef(false);

// Speech result handler (line 72):
if (event.isFinal) {
  setIsListening(false);
  if (text.trim() && !inFlightRef.current && !isSpeaking) {
    handleSend(text.trim());
  }
}

// Top of handleSend:
const handleSend = useCallback(async (userText) => {
  if (inFlightRef.current) return;
  inFlightRef.current = true;
  setIsProcessing(true);
  try {
    // ...existing body...
  } finally {
    inFlightRef.current = false;
    setIsProcessing(false); // move the setIsProcessing(false) here, covers all paths
  }
}, [messages]);

// Mic button Pressable — add disabled prop:
<Pressable
  onPress={handleMicPress}
  disabled={isProcessing}
  style={[styles.micButton, { backgroundColor: micBg }]}
>
```

This also resolves L3 (stale closure only matters under concurrent-send conditions).

### M1 — ChatScreen.js:132 — Await the final message write

Move the AsyncStorage write out of the setState updater so it can be awaited:

```javascript
// In onDone callback (line 129):
(finalText) => {
  setMessages(prev => {
    const final = prev.map(m => m.id === captainMsgId ? { ...m, text: finalText } : m);
    // Kick off the save but return immediately
    return final;
  });
  // Await outside the setState callback (this runs after the render)
  AsyncStorage.setItem(STORAGE_KEY, JSON.stringify([])) // temp; see note
},
```

Actually the cleanest fix is to store the messages in a ref after the functional update and then persist:

```javascript
(finalText) => {
  let finalMessages;
  setMessages(prev => {
    finalMessages = prev.map(m => m.id === captainMsgId ? { ...m, text: finalText } : m);
    return finalMessages;
  });
  // finalMessages is synchronously available after setMessages
  if (finalMessages) {
    AsyncStorage.setItem(STORAGE_KEY, JSON.stringify(finalMessages)).catch(() => {});
  }
},
```

The `.catch(() => {})` handles the write error explicitly (rather than silently).

### M2 — ChatScreen.js:139,152 — Guard settings parse independently

```javascript
// BEFORE (line 139)
const voiceOn = savedSettings ? JSON.parse(savedSettings).voiceEnabled !== false : true;

// AFTER
let voiceOn = true;
if (savedSettings) {
  try { voiceOn = JSON.parse(savedSettings).voiceEnabled !== false; } catch {}
}
```

Apply same pattern at line 152. This way a corrupt settings string doesn't bubble to the outer catch and trigger a spurious re-send.

---

## 5. What's Working Well

- **SSE streaming with defensive callbacks.** The streaming reader catches per-chunk JSON errors and relies on native network teardown for dropped connections; setState callbacks intentionally don't throw. Deliberate design, not an accident.
- **Settings propagate cleanly after `resetClient()`.** Streaming and TTS read AsyncStorage directly; non-streaming rebuilds via `getClient()` on the next call. No split-brain between cached client and stored credentials.
- **Legacy LAN URL self-healing.** `getClient()` actively migrates stale `192.168.x` URLs out of storage. The pattern shows awareness of bad persisted state; it just needs to be applied consistently in `sendMessageStream` too.
- **Threat model correctly scoped.** HTTPS enforcement, URL-injection, and key-extraction concerns are intentionally not over-engineered for a personal single-user tool.
- **Recoverable UI on hang.** Even in the H1/H2 hang scenario, the mic button's `isSpeaking` branch gives a manual escape hatch — the failure degrades rather than bricks the screen.

## 6. Prioritized Fix Roadmap

**Fix now (HIGH — real crash/hang risk)**
1. **H1** — TTS watchdog (20 min) — also closes H2, M4, M5
2. **H3 + H4** — JSON.parse guards (10 min each) — prevents launch crashes
3. **H5** — credentials rollback on failed test (15 min)
4. **H6** — `inFlightRef` + mic `disabled` (20 min) — also closes L3, M3 partially

**Fix this sprint (MEDIUM — robustness)**
- M1 — await the final-message AsyncStorage write
- M2 — guard settings parse independently so it can't trigger re-sends

**Backlog (LOW)**
- L1 — rotate the API key to a value not in source
- L2 — call `AsyncStorage.removeItem` in `sendMessageStream` for parity with `getClient()`
- dep: upgrade `axios` to 1.7.x+ (only runtime-relevant dep vuln)

**Accept as known risk**
- shell-quote / ws / form-data dep vulns — dev/build-only transitive dependencies; not reachable from runtime
- No HTTPS enforcement, URL-injection, `Linking.openURL` — single-user, only Mike sets these values; no threat actor

## 7. Behavior Changes Introduced by Fixes

- **H1 watchdog:** `speak()` will always resolve within the timeout ceiling (60s). A real clip longer than 60s would un-block the UI early while audio continues playing. Set the ceiling above the longest expected TTS output, or compute it from `status.durationMillis` for precision.
- **H3/H4:** Corrupt storage is silently discarded; the user loses that history/settings rather than seeing a crash. Intended, but it is a silent data reset — consider adding an Alert to inform Mike if this happens in practice.
- **H5 (option A — rollback):** Failed test restores previous working credentials automatically. Recommended default. **(Option B — test-only flow):** `handleTest` never persists; a separate Save button is required. Choose one before shipping; they differ in UX.
- **H6:** The mic button becomes visually disabled during processing, and a concurrent send (voice or text) while one is in flight is now silently dropped. Previously the app would attempt the second send. This is the intended behavior but is user-visible.
- **M1:** The final-message write now has explicit error handling. No user-visible change; error is swallowed with `.catch(() => {})` rather than silently lost.

## 8. What's Not Verified

- **Runtime reproduction.** No findings were confirmed by running the app or triggering the hang/crash paths empirically. H1 in particular depends on expo-av's actual `setOnPlaybackStatusUpdate` behavior on a corrupt blob, which was not observed in a live session.
- **expo-av version specifics.** Whether `status.error` is the correct error-detection field for the installed expo-av version was not verified against the lockfile — confirm in expo-av docs for the exact installed version.
- **Backend behavior.** The `callova.live/captain` SSE format, error responses, and auth handling were not inspected; findings assume client-side contract only.
- **iOS vs Android divergence.** Audio session and AsyncStorage behavior can differ by platform; no platform-split testing was done.
- **npm audit currency.** Exact installed versions and whether fixes are available without breaking Expo 54 were not independently confirmed.
